GenoSim 1.0.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Introduction to GenoSim: Forward-Time Genotype Simulation

Compute Population Diversity Metrics Per Generation

Source: R/analysis.R
diversity_metrics.Rd

Computes Nei's gene diversity, proportion of segregating sites, and Watterson's theta estimator for each generation in a simulation result.

diversity_metrics(sim_result)

Arguments

sim_result

A list returned by simulate_population or simulate_from_pedigree.

Value

A data.frame with columns generation, nei_gene_div, seg_sites_frac, and watterson_theta.

References

Nei M (1973) Analysis of gene diversity in subdivided populations. PNAS 70:3321???3323.

See also

compute_fst

Examples

sim <- simulate_population(n_founders=200, n_snps=1000, n_generations=5, seed=1)
#> === GenoSim: Population Forward Simulator ===
#>   Founders: 200 | SNPs: 1000 | Generations: 5
#>   F=0.000 | mu=1.00e-04 | s=0.000 | Ne=200
#> [1/4] Simulating founder genotypes...
#> [2/4] Running forward simulation...
#>   -> Generation 1/5
#>   -> Generation 2/5
#>   -> Generation 3/5
#>   -> Generation 4/5
#>   -> Generation 5/5
#> [3/4] Computing summary statistics...
#> 
#> === Simulation Complete ===
#>   Total individuals: 4151 | SNPs: 1000
div <- diversity_metrics(sim)
print(div)
#>   generation nei_gene_div seg_sites_frac watterson_theta
#> 1          0      0.37072              1         0.17027
#> 2          1      0.36854              1         0.15925
#> 3          2      0.36823              1         0.14958
#> 4          3      0.36943              1         0.14102
#> 5          4      0.36798              1         0.13341
#> 6          5      0.36836              1         0.12658
